content.problemStatement:
JJ loves playing with averages. He has an array AAA of length NNN. He wants to divide the array AAA into two **non-empty** subsets PPP and QQQ such that the value of mean(P)+mean(Q)mean(P) + mean(Q)mean(P)+mean(Q) is as large as possible. (Note that each AiA_iAi​ must belong to either subset PPP or subset QQQ).

Help him find this maximum value of mean(P)+mean(Q)mean(P) + mean(Q)mean(P)+mean(Q).

As a reminder, the mean of a subset XXX of size MMM is defined as: mean(X)=∑i=1MXiMmean(X) = \dfrac{\sum_{i = 1}^{M} X_i}{M}mean(X)=M∑i=1M​Xi​​.

For example, mean([3,1,4,5])=3+1+4+54=3.25mean([3, 1, 4, 5]) = \frac{3 + 1 + 4 + 5}{4} = 3.25mean([3,1,4,5])=43+1+4+5​=3.25.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
Output the largest value of mean(P)+mean(Q)mean(P) + mean(Q)mean(P)+mean(Q).


Your answer is considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.


Formally, let your answer be aaa, and the jury's answer be bbb. Your answer is accepted if and only if ∣a−b∣max⁡(1,∣b∣)≤10−6\frac{|a - b|}{\max{(1, |b|)}} \le 10^{-6}max(1,∣b∣)∣a−b∣​≤10−6.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (the size of the array). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible value of mean(P) + mean(Q). The answer must be printed with an absolute or relative error of at most 1e-6 (typically six digits after the decimal point).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N=2, the only possible partition is {4} and {5}. mean(P)+mean(Q)=4+5=9.\nTest case 2: All elements are equal to 2, any partition gives mean(P)=mean(Q)=2, so the sum is 4.
Sorted descending: [3,2,1], total=6. For k=1 (largest element 3) we get 3/1 + 3/2 = 4.5, which is maximal.
Choosing P={10} (k=1) gives 10 + (3)/3 = 11, which is the best possible.

solution.approach:
Sort the array in non‑increasing order. Let S be the total sum. For a subset P of size k (1 ≤ k < N) the value to maximise is
    f(k, Sp) = Sp/k + (S‑Sp)/(N‑k)
where Sp is the sum of elements placed in P. For a fixed k the expression is linear in Sp with coefficient c = 1/k – 1/(N‑k). If c > 0 (i.e. k < N/2) we should maximise Sp, therefore we take the k largest elements. If c < 0 (k > N/2) we should minimise Sp, which is equivalent to taking the N‑k largest elements for the other subset – the same values are obtained by evaluating the case k' = N‑k (< N/2). When N is even and k = N/2 the coefficient is zero, the value is constant (2·S/N). Consequently it is enough to consider only k = 1 … ⌊N/2⌋, compute the prefix sums of the sorted array, and evaluate
    f = pref[k]/k + (S‑pref[k])/(N‑k).
The maximum over all k is the answer.
Complexities: sorting O(N log N), scanning O(N). Memory O(N) for the prefix sums.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case because of sorting; the scan over k is O(N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array and its prefix sums.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        total = sum(arr)
        arr.sort(reverse=True)
        pref = 0
        best = 0.0
        limit = N // 2
        for k in range(1, limit + 1):
            pref += arr[k-1]
            cur = pref / k + (total - pref) / (N - k)
            if cur > best:
                best = cur
        out_lines.append(f"{best:.6f}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
